返回本批次

Allbirds 宣布从鞋子转向人工智能，股价暴涨 175%

Allbirds announces pivot from shoes to AI, stock explodes 175%

www.cnbc.com 作者 1vuio0pswjnm7 分数 95 评论 33 2026/04/15 21:34
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 运动鞋制造商 Allbirds 联合创始人兼联席董事长蒂姆·布朗 (Tim Brown) 于 2023 年 5 月 10 日在 OMR Festival 数字贸易展上登台演讲。图片联盟| Allbirds 周三令人惊讶地宣布，公司正在从鞋子转向人工智能。此举使这家市值极小的公司的股价上涨了 582%（周二收盘时该公司的估值约为 2100 万美元）。该股一天前还不到 3 美元，现已跃升至约 17 美元。该公司在其投资者关系页面上发布的一份新闻稿中宣布，其业务将转向人工智能计算基础设施。根据新闻稿，该公司将被称为 NewBird AI，宣布了一项筹集高达 5000 万美元资金的交易，预计将于 2026 年第二季度完成。“该公司最初将寻求收购高性能、低延迟的人工智能计算硬件，并根据长期租赁安排提供访问权限，该公司在公告中表示：“满足现货市场和超大规模企业无法可靠服务的客户需求。” American Exchange Group 是一家专注于配饰领域的品牌管理公司。据新闻稿称，它将继续以 Allbirds 品牌销售产品。 Allbirds 在 2 月份关闭了美国所有全价商店。 阅读更多 CNBC 科技新闻 律师表示，奥特曼纵火嫌疑人莫雷诺-伽马遭受了“严重的心理健康危机” NAACP 因孟菲斯数据中心空气污染问题起诉埃隆·马斯克的 xAI 随着 Hock Tan 决定离开董事会，Meta 承诺与博通合作 1 吉瓦的定制芯片 Nvidia 股票连续 10 天上涨，在此期间上涨了 18% 该公司自 OpenAI 于 2022 年推出 ChatGPT 聊天机器人以来，人工智能热潮在华尔街引发了一阵热潮。人工智能基础设施是一项众所周知的昂贵且复杂的业务，但它可能利润丰厚。图形处理器市场的主导者英伟达(Nvidia)已迅速成长为世界上最有价值的公司，市值接近5万亿美元。股票市场上有陷入困境的公司转向当前热门行业以吸引兴趣的历史。在比特币繁荣期间，多家公司宣布与区块链合作或直接转换为加密货币公司，以重新激发人们对其股票的兴趣。 Allbirds 由前职业足球运动员 Tim Brown 和可再生资源专家 Joey Zwillinger 于 2015 年创立，旨在创造一种依赖天然材料而不是塑料和其他石油产品的新鞋类。 2016 年，Allbirds 推出了首款由美利奴羊毛制成的鞋子，并立即获得成功，特别是在被该品牌的舒适性和可持续性所吸引的“科技兄弟”中。该公司启动了一项雄心勃勃的开店计划，并于 2021 年上市，但很快就发现，随着趋势的变化、竞争对手的加入以及客户获取成本的上升，其业务开始放缓。2022 年至 2025 年，销售额暴跌近 50%——从2.98 亿美元至 1.52 亿美元。Stock Chart IconStock Chart iconAllbirds 一日股票图表。— CNBC 的 Ashley Capoot 对本报告做出了贡献。

Original Summary

  1. Tim Brown, co-founder and co-chairman of sports shoe manufacturer Allbirds, speaks on stage at the OMR Festival digital trade show, May 10, 2023.Marcus Brandt | Picture Alliance | Getty ImagesAllbirds made a surprising announcement Wednesday that it is pivoting from shoes to artificial intelligence. The move boosted shares of the miniscule market cap company — it was valued at about $21 million at Tuesday's close — by 582%. The shares, which were under $3 a day ago, jumped to about $17.The company announced that it's pivoting its business to AI compute infrastructure in a release posted to its investor relations page.The company, which according to the release will be called NewBird AI, announced a deal to raise up to $50 million in funding, expected to close in the second quarter of 2026."The Company will initially seek to acquire high-performance, low-latency AI compute hardware and provide access under long-term lease arrangements, meeting customer demand that spot markets and hyperscalers are unable to reliably service," the company said in the announcement.watch nowAllbirds, once a Wall Street darling valued north of $4 billion, announced a deal with American Exchange Group to sell its intellectual property and other assets for $39 million last month. American Exchange Group is a brand management company focused on the accessory space. According to the release, it will continue to sell products under the Allbirds brand. Allbirds closed all its U.S. full-priced stores in February.Read more CNBC tech newsAltman arson suspect Moreno-Gama suffered 'acute mental health crisis,' lawyer saysNAACP sues Elon Musk's xAI over Memphis data center air pollutionMeta commits to 1 GW of custom chips with Broadcom as Hock Tan decides to leave boardNvidia stock is on a 10-day winning streak and up 18% over that stretchThe company is the latest firm looking to cash in on the AI boom, which has ignited a fever on Wall Street since OpenAI launched its ChatGPT chatbot in 2022. AI infrastructure is a notoriously expensive and complex business, but it can be lucrative. Nvidia, which dominates the market for graphics processing units, has ballooned into the most valuable company in the world, with a market cap that's approaching $5 trillion.There's a history in the stock market of troubled companies pivoting to the hot industry of the moment in order to garner interest. During the bitcoin boom, several companies announced a blockchain tie-in or converted outright to a cryptocurrency company to reignite interest in their stock.Allbirds was founded in 2015 by former professional soccer player Tim Brown and renewable resources expert Joey Zwillinger to create a new category of shoes that relied on natural materials, not plastics and other petroleum products. In 2016, Allbirds introduced its debut shoe, made with merino wool, and became an instant success, particularly among "tech bros" who were drawn to the brand's comfort and sustainability.It embarked on an ambitious store opening plan and went public in 2021, but soon saw its business begin to slow as trends changed, competitors moved in and customer acquisition costs rose.Between 2022 and 2025, sales plummeted nearly 50% — falling from $298 million to $152 million.Stock Chart IconStock chart iconAllbirds one-day stock chart.— CNBC's Ashley Capoot contributed to this report.

评论（默认中文）

mikeve 2026/04/15 23:03
这看起来几乎就像一个SPAC。 Allbirds 已经出售了其知识产权和其他资产，现在他们筹集了新资金，并以不同的名称继续经营，但他们仍然是一家上市公司。

因此，这并不是真正的鞋类零售商转向人工智能，而是一家鞋类零售商出售所有资产并组建一家新公司，但使用之前的公开股票上市。

This appears to be almost like a SPAC. Allbirds had already sold it's IP and other assets, now they raised new money and are continuing under a different name but they're still a publicly traded company.

So it's not really shoe retailer pivots to AI but a shoe retailer selling all assets and forming a new company but uses the previous public stock listing.

在 HN 中查看
kermatt 2026/04/16 01:03
那么，只是创办一家完全不同的新公司，但却使用完全不同业务的旧品牌名称？
So just starting a new and completely different company, but using an old brand name from a completely different business?
在 HN 中查看
presbyterian 2026/04/16 01:09
他们甚至没有使用相同的品牌名称；他们卖掉了 Allbirds 的名字，现在更名为“Newbird AI”。我猜只是为了留在交易所？
They're not even using the same brand name; they sold the Allbirds name and are now "Newbird AI". I guess it's just to stay on the exchange?
在 HN 中查看
garethsprice 2026/04/16 02:45
这是我的赌注——申请首次公开募股甚至直接上市需要花费数月的美国证券交易委员会审查、审计、承销商、尽职调查等时间。拥有现有股票代码的空壳可以让你几乎立即获得散户投资者的丰厚现金，并且不会有那些烦人的监管机构询问你是否真的拥有一个正常运作的产品或公司。

与 SPAC 相比，这更像是反向合并（例如，伯克希尔哈撒韦是一家失败的纺织厂，而 WPP 是一家金属丝篮公司），只不过这种情况在现有领导团队中发生是不寻常的。我猜他们卖掉了所有的知识产权，因此认为他们不妨利用这个外壳来尝试从人工智能炒作中获利。

This would be my bet - filing for an IPO or even direct listing is months of SEC review, audits, underwriters, due diligence, etc. A shell with an existing ticker gets you access to that sweet retail investor cash almost immediately, and with less of those pesky regulators asking if you actually have a functioning product or company.

This is more akin to a reverse merger than a SPAC (eg. Berkshire Hathaway being a failing textile mill and WPP being a wire basket company) except it is unusual to see it happen within an existing leadership team. They sold off all their IP so figured they might as well use the shell to try and cash in on AI hype, I guess.

在 HN 中查看
Tadpole9181 2026/04/16 03:18
您能向我解释一下为什么这不被视为彻头彻尾的欺诈吗？美国证券交易委员会针对公司/票据的生命周期，在纳入指数和退休账户等方面制定了专门的规则，对吧？

允许这种情况怎么会不简单地鼓励一个基本上是股市业力农耕的世界：几乎不制造公司公司，只是为了出售门票，这样他们就可以用工人阶级仅剩的一点财富来参与拉高和抛售计划？

这个领域不完全是我的强项...

Can you explain to me how this isn't considered outright fraud? The SEC has rules specifically around the lifetime of a company / ticket in terms of things like inclusion in indexes and retirement accounts, right?

How does allowing this not simply encourage a world of, basically, stock market karma farming: making bullshit barely company companies, just to sell the ticket so they can be used to engage in a pump and dump scheme with the little wealth the working class has left?

This domain isn't exactly my forte...

在 HN 中查看
floatrock 2026/04/16 02:10
基本上。

他们以 3900 万美元的价格出售了 Allbirds 品牌和知识产权：

> 该公司巅峰时期估值约为 40 亿美元，两周前以 3900 万美元的价格出售了其知识产权和其他资产。

...利用股票代码进行股票炒作，这将带来另外 5000 万美元的收入：

> 根据新闻稿，该公司将被称为 NewBird AI，宣布了一项筹集至多 5000 万美元资金的交易，预计将于 2026 年第二季度完成。

...并计划利用这 9000 万美元的资金向最新炒作市场中的边际买家出售一些额外的铲子。

> 该公司在公告中表示：“公司最初将寻求获取高性能、低延迟的人工智能计算硬件，并根据长期租赁安排提供访问权限，以满足现货市场和超大规模企业无法可靠服务的客户需求。”

这实际上只是把尸体捡干净......公司损失了 99% 的价值，从 $4B 跌至 $0.04B。这只是将所剩无几的资本重新投入到可能为剩下的持有股票的人带来少量回报的事情上。

Basically.

They sold the Allbirds brand and IP for $39M:

> The company, valued at around $4 billion at its peak, sold its intellectual property and other assets two weeks ago for $39 million.

...used the ticker to build stock hype that will bring in another $50M:

> The company, which according to the release will be called NewBird AI, announced a deal to raise up to $50 million in funding, expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.

...and is planning on using that $90M of capital to sell a few extra shovels to the marginal buyer in the latest hype market.

> “The Company will initially seek to acquire high-performance, low-latency AI compute hardware and provide access under long-term lease arrangements, meeting customer demand that spot markets and hyperscalers are unable to reliably service,” the company said in the announcement.

This is really just picking the corpse clean... company lost 99% of value, dropping from $4B to $0.04B. This is just redirecting what little capital is left into something that might get a small return for whoever is left holding shares.

在 HN 中查看
holtkam2 2026/04/16 01:15
有人告诉我我的想法是否愚蠢：

您知道在您的公司名称/域名中添加“.ai”如何使您的估值提高一个数量级或其他什么吗？

那么有人尝试过吗 链接 .ai 是他们公司的名称......？？？

例如，x.ai 通过更名为 x.ai.ai 是否可以将其估值再提高 5-10 倍？

perplexity.ai.ai.ai 怎么样？

增加对贵公司的估值/关注度似乎是一个唾手可得的成果，但如果我遗漏了什么，请告诉我。

Someone tell me if my idea is dumb:

You know how adding ".ai" to your company name / domain name increases your valuation an order of magnitude or whatever?

Well has anyone tried chaining .ai's to their company name...???

For example could x.ai increase their valuation another 5-10x by renaming themselves x.ai.ai?

What about perplexity.ai.ai.ai?

Seems like a low-hanging fruit for increasing valuation / attention on your company, but let me know if I'm missing something.

apparent 2026/04/16 01:58
当旧名称是复数时，新名称是单数（“Newbird AI”），这似乎很奇怪。想知道他们是如何选择的。我无法想象“Newbirds AI”被拿走了！
Seems weird the new name is singular ("Newbird AI") when the old name is plural. Wonder how they chose that. I can't imagine "Newbirds AI" was taken!

eudamoniac 2026/04/15 23:45
令人沮丧的是，施瓦布不允许我购买该品种的看跌期权，而且我也没有启用卖空保证金，因为我总是购买看跌期权。我一生中从未见过如此清晰的短片。
It's frustrating that Schwab does not allow me to buy puts for this symbol, and I haven't enabled margin for shorting because I always bought puts instead. Never seen a clearer short in my life.
在 HN 中查看
ProjectArcturis 2026/04/16 00:44
不仅仅是嘉信理财，BIRD 上没有任何期权交易。做空并不能保证。首先你必须找到一笔借款，如果可以的话，今天的年化成本可能>1000%。然后你必须能够维持借用，即使 BIRD 从这里加倍。

根据今天的价格走势，这似乎是轧空。

It's not just Schwab, there aren't any options traded on BIRD. And shorting is not a guarantee. First you have to find a borrow, which if it's available probably costs >1000% annualized today. Then you have to be able to maintain that borrow, even if BIRD doubles from here.

Based on the price action today, this seems like a short squeeze.

therobots927 2026/04/16 02:17
4 月 1 日左右，该股票的交易量出现了高度异常的峰值： https://marketchameleon.com/Overview/BIRD/Summary/

奇怪的是，当时的价格几乎没有变化。我认为可以公平地说这是一系列高度可疑的事件。

There was a highly anomalous spike in volume around April 1st in the stock: https://marketchameleon.com/Overview/BIRD/Summary/

Oddly enough it barely moved the price at the time. I think it’s fair to say that this is a highly suspicious sequence of events.

SideburnsOfDoom 2026/04/15 22:59
我不是对此的判断者，但情况如何可能是一个指标——即这只股票的上涨、抛售和崩盘在多长时间内发生。

PS：谷歌搜索“allbirds破产”表明不，这家公司做得不好。很多“出了什么问题”的文章，

和

https://wwd.com/footwear-news/shoe-industry-news/allbirds-ip...

https://www.businessoffashion.com/news/retail/allbirds-to-be...

I am not the judge of that, but how this goes might be an indicator - i.e. over what timespan the pump, dump, crash of this stock plays out.

PS: googling "allbirds bankrupt" shows that no, this company is not doing well. Lots of "What went wrong" articles,

and

https://wwd.com/footwear-news/shoe-industry-news/allbirds-ip...

https://www.businessoffashion.com/news/retail/allbirds-to-be...
